item.title: When Do Engineered Features Beat Raw Prices? A Walk-Forward Benchmark of Classical, Neural, and World-Model Predictors on a Global Equity Dataset
item.description:
Highlights

  • Built a walk-forward backtesting framework benchmarking classical, neural, and world-model predictors on a global equity dataset.
  • Isolated the conditions under which engineered features outperform raw price inputs across model families.

item.title: SAGE: Scalable Agentic Grounded Evaluation for Crop Disease Diagnosis
item.description:
Highlights

  • Co-built the largest plant-disease image–symptom dataset to date: 335 crops, 1,251 disease classes, ~839K images with source-grounded, expert-validated symptom descriptions.
  • Engineered stepwise diagnostic reasoning (anatomical context → symptom-guided narrowing → reference-image comparison → explainable trace).
  • Symptom grounding improved accuracy by +16.2 points on average and extends to new crops with no retraining.
  • Core component of M.S. thesis research (co-first author).

item.title: TRAPS: Therapeutic Response Analysis via Pathway-informed Stratification
item.description:
Highlights

  • Built the first unified benchmark for pathway-guided cancer therapy-response modeling (BINN, GraphPath, PATH) under identical conditions.
  • Multi-task pipeline over 2,622 TCGA patients across 5 cohorts using Reactome pathway-activity features.
  • Best model reached 0.92 AUROC on prostate targeted-therapy prediction at 11% class prevalence.

item.title: Improving the Safety and Trustworthiness of Medical AI via Multi-Agent Evaluation Loops
item.description:
Highlights

  • Built a multi-agent iterative-refinement loop: generators DeepSeek R1 + Med-PaLM, evaluators LLaMA 3.1 + Phi-4.
  • Aligned outputs to AMA Principles of Medical Ethics and a five-tier Safety Risk Assessment (SRA-5).
  • Cut ethical violations by 89% and achieved a 92% risk-downgrade rate over 900 clinical queries across nine ethical domains.

item.title: Intelligent Health Intervention System for Syndemic Management Among University Students: An AI-Driven Approach Using Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Highlights

  • Co-developed a reinforcement-learning intervention engine for syndemic health management.
  • Modeled intervention timing and selection as a sequential decision problem.

item.title: Interpreting Transformers through the Lens of Physics and Dynamical Systems
item.description:
Highlights

  • First-authored an analysis interpreting transformer architectures through physics and dynamical-systems theory to explain their internal computation dynamics.

item.title: An Agentic Framework for Mechanistic Therapeutic Reasoning in Oncology via Pathway-Grounded Tree-of-Thought Inference
item.description:
Highlights

  • Designed an agentic, pathway-grounded tree-of-thought inference pipeline structuring LLM reasoning over biological pathway knowledge for oncology therapeutic reasoning.

item.title: LlamaHealth: Assessing LLaMA's Performance in Multi-Class Healthcare Test Result Prediction
item.description:
Highlights

  • Benchmarked LLaMA on multi-class healthcare test-result prediction.
  • Assessed reliability for responsible medical-AI deployment.

item.title: Benchmarking Bioemu-1: Establishing a Baseline for Protein Structural Ensemble Prediction
item.description:
Highlights

  • Established baseline benchmarks for Bioemu-1 on protein structural-ensemble prediction.
  • Defined reproducible metrics for computational structural biology.

item.title: Emissai: LLM-Powered Analysis of Carbon Footprint
item.description:
Highlights

  • Built an LLM-powered tool to estimate and analyze carbon footprint from operational data.

item.title: Federated In-Context Prompt Selection for Multi-Modal 3D Dental Imaging: A Theoretical Framework with Privacy-Preserving Guarantees
item.description:
Highlights

  • Designed a privacy-preserving federated-learning framework for multi-modal 2D/3D dental imaging.
  • Featured cross-modal prompt alignment, hierarchical multi-modal optimization, and Byzantine-resilient aggregation with differential privacy.

item.title: Enhancing Medical AI Safety Through Multi-Agent Evaluation and Iterative Refinement
item.description:
Highlights

  • Prototyped the multi-agent medical-LLM safety framework (generators DeepSeek R1 / Med-PaLM; evaluators LLaMA 3.1 / Phi-4).
  • Aligned to AMA principles and the SRA-5 protocol; later extended into the arXiv study “Improving the Safety and Trustworthiness of Medical AI via Multi-Agent Evaluation Loops”.

item.title: Leveraging Vision Language Models for Specialized Agricultural Tasks
item.description:
Highlights

  • Co-developed AgEval, a 12-task plant-stress phenotyping benchmark for VLMs (Claude, GPT, Gemini, LLaVA).
  • Best-model F1 improved 46.24% → 73.37% (8-shot); strategic example selection added +15.38% F1.
  • Introduced a coefficient-of-variation metric revealing per-class gaps of 26–58%.

item.title: The Impact of Market Orientation and Brand Storytelling on Shark Tank Evaluations
item.description:
Highlights

  • Built a GPT-4o analysis pipeline over 430 Shark Tank pitches.
  • Combined expert annotation with statistical validation to calibrate LLM-derived predictions for a peer-reviewed B2B marketing study.

item.title: Chatbot Frontend Development & UI Enhancement
item.description:
Overview

Collaborated with Professor Ryan McGhee at the ISU Department of Agricultural and Biosystems Engineering to develop a versatile chatbot frontend that significantly improves user experience and interface design.

Key Responsibilities

  • Frontend development and architecture
  • User interface design and enhancement
  • User experience optimization
  • Integration with backend systems
  • Iterative design based on user feedback

Skills Applied

  • Modern web frameworks and technologies
  • Responsive UI design
  • User-centered design principles
  • Software development best practices

item.title: Medical AI Safety & Ethical Alignment
item.description:
Overview

Developed novel approaches to enhance the safety and ethical compliance of medical LLM outputs. This research addresses critical challenges in deploying AI for healthcare:

  • Multi-agent evaluation frameworks using specialized LLMs as evaluator agents
  • Iterative refinement against established healthcare standards (AMA-9 Principles)
  • Safety Risk Assessment (SRA-5) tier implementation
  • Byzantine-resilient aggregation with differential privacy guarantees

Key Achievements

  • ACM-BCB 2025 Presentation - Poster presentation at The 16th ACM Conference on Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, and Health Informatics
  • Developed framework pairing generative models (DeepSeek R1, Med-PaLM) with evaluator agents (LLaMA 3.1, Phi-4)
  • Focus on real-world deployment challenges and regulatory compliance

item.title: Vision-Language Models for Agriculture
item.description:
Overview

Exploring how state-of-the-art vision-language models (VLMs) can be adapted and optimized for specialized agricultural tasks. This research contributes to advancing precision agriculture through AI.

Research Focus

  • Benchmarking VLMs on agricultural datasets
  • Specialized prompt engineering for farm-related tasks
  • Cross-modal understanding of agricultural imagery
  • Real-world deployment in agricultural systems

Publications

  • WACV 2025 - “Leveraging Vision Language Models for Specialized Agricultural Tasks” (IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision)
  • Research on protein structural prediction (Bioemu-1 benchmarking at AIChE 2025)

Applications

  • Crop disease and pest identification
  • Yield estimation
  • Field condition assessment
  • Climate-aware agricultural decision support

item.title: LLMs in Finance: Enhancing Decision-Making Processes
item.description:
Overview

Collaborating with Professor Soumik Sarkar at the Translational AI Center to explore how LLMs can enhance decision-making processes in finance. This research includes:

  • Analyzing how LLMs interpret market signals and business fundamentals
  • Studying investor behavior through the lens of AI analysis (Shark Tank data)
  • Developing frameworks for AI-assisted financial analysis
  • Understanding market orientation and brand storytelling effects on investment decisions

Key Contributions

  • Published research on market orientation and startup pitch evaluation (JBIM, REU 2025)
  • Developed LLM-based analytical frameworks for entrepreneurship and investment analysis
  • Presented findings at TrAC Day 2025 and REU Summer Symposium 2025
